DIA Exception Handling and Notification Flow
	1. Objective
Design the exception-handling flow for the DIA post-ingestion processing chain covering missing file detection, corrupt file handling, decryption failure, SharePoint save failure, notification behavior, and retry applicability. 
	2. Scope
This design covers the processing flow after file ingestion, including: 
	• expected file arrival monitoring 
	• file validation/corruption detection 
	• decryption failure handling 
	• SharePoint save failure handling 
	• notification routing 
	• retry classification 
	• clarification of duplicate or ambiguous requirements 
	3. Related Requirements 
	• Req 43: file not received at expected time 
	• Req 44: received file is corrupt 
	• Req 45: failed to decrypt DIA file 
	• Req 46: failed to save decrypted DIA file to SharePoint 
	• Req 47: appears duplicate of Req 45 and requires clarification 
	4. High-Level Flow 
	• system expects DIA file via SFTP by configured schedule 
	• if file is not received by cut-off time, trigger notification flow 
	• if file is received, validate file integrity/readability 
	• if file is corrupt, trigger notification flow 
	• if file is valid, attempt decryption 
	• if decryption fails, trigger notification flow 
	• if decryption succeeds, attempt save to designated SharePoint folder 
	• if SharePoint save fails, trigger notification flow 
	• if all steps succeed, continue downstream staging/processing 
	
	5. Scenario Breakdown
Scenario 1
Missing expected DIA file 
	• likely requires timer/scheduled monitoring logic 
	• not normal retryable processing 
	• notify Collections Leaders, copy Estate Guardians 
Scenario 2
Corrupt DIA file
	• file received but invalid/unreadable 
	• terminal for that file 
	• notify Collections Leaders, copy Estate Guardians 
Scenario 3
DIA decryption failure
	• file received but decryption fails 
	• retry only if transient cause exists 
	• notify relevant Technology team / Estate Guardians 
Scenario 4
SharePoint save failure
	• file decrypted successfully but save/upload fails 
	• likely retryable if transient 
	• notify relevant Technology team / Estate Guardians 
Scenario 5
Duplicate decryption requirement
	• Req 47 appears same as Req 45 
	• clarification needed before implementation 
	6. Retry Classification
Non-retry scenarios 
	• missing file by expected time 
	• corrupt file 
	• permanent decryption failure 
Conditional retry scenarios
	• transient decryption failure 
	• SharePoint save failure caused by temporary access/connectivity/service issue 
	7. Technical Considerations 
	• expected-time rule must be confirmed 
	• corruption criteria must be defined 
	• arrival-tracking mechanism must be identified 
	• SharePoint access/security/permissions/path handling must be confirmed 
	• email routing and trigger timing must be confirmed 
	• logging/traceability should be included to support supportability and investigation 
	8. Open Clarifications 
	• what is the exact cut-off time or monitoring rule for Req 43 
	• where should file arrival status be checked or recorded 
	• how is “corrupt” technically defined 
	• is Req 47 really duplicate of Req 45 
	• should email be sent immediately or only after retry exhaustion for retryable failures 
what is the final routing for the relevant Technology team
